name: Chromium Android ScrollCapture
version: Chromium main; scroll-capture support introduced in 2021 and maintained
project_status: Active feature with ongoing maintenance and tests
capture_driver: Chrome binds ScrollCaptureCallback to the current tab and generates long-screenshot tiles from web content using the paint-preview pipeline
compose_support: No Compose-specific support; targets browser web content
view_support: Chrome tabs / WebContents, not arbitrary Android views
stitching_strategy: Tile-based capture from rendered web content
matching_algorithm: None; it uses rendered tiles rather than image matching
scroll_offset_trust: Uses WebContents render coordinates, page scale, and scroll position to map capture areas
duplicate_frame_handling: No explicit dedupe; empty rects or cancelation are used when capture is unavailable
overlap_requirement: No fixed overlap; bounded by tile capture geometry
confidence_fallback: Returns an empty rect if WebContents is missing or frozen, and cancels on bitmap-generation failure
static_chrome_handling: Targets the web page content area, keeping browser chrome outside the capture path
dynamic_content_robustness: Limited by page changes, frozen tabs, and unsupported subscrollers
content_type_fitness: Good for long web articles, feeds, and chats
memory_strategy: On-demand bitmap generation with surface-backed tile capture
max_output_constraints: Bounded by page size, viewport geometry, and Android tile buffer limits
output_format: Rendered bitmap tiles posted through ScrollCaptureSession
oem_compatibility: Depends on Android ScrollCapture support and OEM WebView or browser behavior
integration: Chrome share module registers ScrollCaptureCallbackImpl and ScrollCaptureManager for the current tab
windowing_behavior: Captures the scrollable content area of the active tab; subscrollers are not yet fully supported
data_path: Fully on-device in the browser and SystemUI pipeline
permissions_required: No special runtime permission beyond Android ScrollCapture support
chart_stitching_fitness: Good for static web charts, but subscroller limits apply
chat_stitching_fitness: Good for long web chat or article pages
